title: quadrella calciphila
intro: Quadrella calciphila, a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, is a member of the Capparaceae family and is commonly known as the Mexican poppy tree. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Quadrella calciphila include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even treat certain types of cancer. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in capsule or powder form for its medicinal purposes.
